name: Cannelloni (Stuffed Pasta)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine flour and salt. Add eggs and salad oil; mix with a fork until a firm dough forms.
2. Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for about 5 minutes until smooth.
3. Cover and let rest for 5 minutes.
4. Roll out the dough into a rectangle measuring 12x18 inches, then cut into eighteen 4x3-inch rectangles.
5. Cover and let rest for 1 hour.
6. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the pasta rectangles in batches for about 5 minutes or until tender. Drain in cold water and pat with a damp towel.
7. Meanwhile, prepare the sauce:
A. Sauté onion, garlic, and basil in butter until tender.
B. Add tomatoes; bring to a boil.
C. Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
8. Pour 1 cup of sauce into the bottom of a 3-quart, 13x9x2-inch baking dish.
9. Prepare the filling:
A. Mix soup, milk, and Parmesan cheese in a bowl.
B. Combine half of the soup mixture with remaining ingredients in a saucepan; cook until thickened.
10. Assemble cannelloni:
A. Place 2 tablespoons of filling at the narrow end of each pasta piece and roll up.
B. Arrange roll-ups, seam-side down, in the baking dish. Top with remaining soup mixture and sauce.
11. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 30 to 35 minutes or until hot.
12. Serve with additional Parmesan cheese.
